introduction: In 1946, Soviet-supported Azeri-Iranians established an autonomous republic under the Azerbaijan People's Government. The republic lasted about a year, dissolving after the Soviets withdrew. Azeri soldiers—many of them teenagers—were forced to flee to other locations in the USSR. Most of these exiles were then arrested and deported to Siberian camps. "According to some sources, Stalin ultimately perceived the integration of the Ferqeh activists into the Azerbaijan SSR as a threat and as a potential source of political unrest."This documentary interviews several former participants in the "Azerbaijan Crisis" from their homes in exile, from Kazakhstan to Sweden.An interesting document of oral histories, and a dive into nationalisms, exile, and the effects of WWII on Iran.
